flashcards.back: A reflection model where light is scattered equally in all directions, characteristic of matte surfaces. Appearance is independent of viewing angle.
The nonlinear function that maps incident light (irradiance × exposure time) to digital pixel values in a camera.

flashcards.front: What is the difference between radiometry and photometry?
flashcards.back: Radiometry measures physical light properties, while photometry measures light as perceived by the human eye, weighted by spectral sensitivity.
The phenomenon where image corners appear darker than the center due to the cos⁴θ term in the image irradiance equation.

flashcards.front: What is the purpose of the Bayer pattern?
flashcards.back: To capture color information using a single sensor with a mosaic of color filters (typically 50% green, 25% red, 25% blue pixels).
